marketState: neutral, mixed crosscurrents - bearish near-term technicals vs bullish weather/COT setup 3+ weeks out
summary: NG front month is trading almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.912 vs $2.919 fair, spread -$0.007), so there is no material valuation edge right now. Seasonality is soft on the surface (-4.9pp vs median, YTD -14.5% vs -9.6% median) but 69% of the time price has historically been higher in 4 weeks (reliability flagged Low, 63pp spread). The 8-16 day weather window shows demand running +35.6% above 5-year normal and above the top of its historical range - a real bullish catalyst for the 2-3 week horizon that fair value barely reflects (only rising to $2.985 by day 21). Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the past 5 years, a crowded position that raises short-squeeze risk if that demand spike hits, while storage sits +183 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise (+17 Bcf), capping fundamental upside. Meanwhile every technical timeframe from 5-minute to weekly is bearish (trend down, 50<200 EMA cross), favoring near-term weakness even as the medium-term setup skews constructive.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; consider taking partial off at TP1
longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is +35.6% vs 5y and above the top of its historical range, a genuine cooling-demand catalyst that fair value has barely priced in yet (rising only to $2.985 by day 21); combined with managed money net short at the 8th 5y percentile (squeeze risk) and 69% historical odds of higher prices in 4 weeks, the medium-term bias leans long despite soft YTD seasonality.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D weather demand anomaly fades back toward 5y normal, or storage surplus (+183 Bcf vs 5y) keeps growing with further bearish surprises, or price closes decisively below $2.75

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.5-1% account risk (reduced vs normal given crowded short COT positioning raises squeeze risk)
shortTerm.rationale: All technical timeframes (5m through 1w) show bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses and mostly negative MACD, and this week's storage build beat forecast by +17 Bcf (bearish surprise); near-term (1-7D) weather demand is only mildly above normal (+3.8%) so it isn't yet offsetting the bearish tape.
shortTerm.invalidation: Price reclaims and holds above $3.05, or near-term weather demand data pulls forward the 8-16D heat spike, or a short-covering rally develops given the crowded managed-money short

keyDrivers: 8-16D weather demand +35.6% vs 5y and above max range - building cooling-demand catalyst
Managed money net short at 8th percentile of 5y - crowded short raises squeeze risk
Storage +183 Bcf vs 5y with bearish +17 Bcf build surprise - caps fundamental upside
Price essentially at fair value across all horizons (spread -$0.007 to -$0.073) - no valuation edge
All technical timeframes (5m-1w) bearish trend with 50<200 EMA cross - near-term downward pressure
risks: Seasonality reliability flagged Low (63pp historical spread) undermines confidence in the seasonal edge
